mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trajectory in the federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's predictive power decays to zero after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production growth accelerates while housing starts decline indicates a supply-demand imbalance favoring corporate earnings but constraining consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between high INDPRO/low HOUST regimes and the baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 2.5% annualized, aggressive Fed rate hikes historically compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when CPI exceeds 2.5% and rates rise, or the signal's IC drops below 0.01 in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding corporate revenue bases, which directly supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months, it signals robust labor demand that fuels consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite the positive signal, or the signal's predictive power decays to zero after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising industrial production and declining unemployment indicates strong capacity utilization alongside tight labor markets, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between high INDPRO/low UNRATE regimes and the baseline.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid credit expansion where RSAFS exceeds its 12-month moving average by more than 10%, equity valuations typically expand due to increased liquidity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when RSAFS exceeds its 12-month MA by 10%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.01 in post-2008 data.
